canonical_definition: Within the provided literature, RADAR is presented as an AI text detection system. Its defined purpose is to identify machine-generated text by leveraging distributional differences in language models. The system is also described as having robust cross-domain performance. In use, it is situated as one of several tools for this task, with one paper evaluating it as part of a "suite of prominent detectors" that also includes GPT-Zero, MAGE, and Binoculars.
papers: MultiMed-ST: Large-scale Many-to-many Multilingual Medical Speech Translation
Social Genome: Grounded Social Reasoning Abilities of Multimodal Models

definitions.text: An AI text detection system that leverages distributional differences in language models to identify machine-generated text with robust cross-domain performance.
definitions.paper: MultiMed-ST: Large-scale Many-to-many Multilingual Medical Speech Translation
